label: hate
rationale: 'Bukusus do not beat kikuyus in alcoholism' attributes collective alcoholism to Kikuyus as a community, even while defending Bukusus - NCIC collective negative characteristic.

label: neither
rationale: Ethnic-bloc fragmentation analysis and political commentary on Ruto's position, no contempt directed at Kalenjins or any community as a whole.

label: neither
rationale: Criticism contrasting two named politicians; 'gakunia' is a personal dismissal of Kuria, not a transfer of contempt onto the Kikuyu community.

label: hate
rationale: 'the uncivilized luo community' attributes collective incivility to Luos as a community - NCIC collective negative characteristic.

label: hate
rationale: 'Argentina is the most racist country in the world... killed all natives & blacks' attributes collective murderous racism to an entire national-origin group - collective negative characteristic under NCIC standard.

label: neither
rationale: Reporting a specific incident of violence by an MP's goons against Kenyans; no ethnic contempt, no group attack.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus will do nothing. Payback time' frames the 2027 swearing-in as ethnic retribution against Kikuyus collectively for 2007, combining collective blame with an implied threat.

label: offensive
rationale: Text appears to be isiZulu or similar language; best reading is crude personal abuse directed at an individual ('sgora sashibhoshi' - ugly face), no clear ethnic group targeting.

label: hate
rationale: 'Luos won't give us rest on these streets. They are all over' attributes a collective, homogeneous irritating behaviour to Luos as a community, not criticism of a named individual.

label: neither
rationale: 'demon the sugoi guy is' is a personal insult aimed at Ruto as an individual politician, not transferred onto any ethnic community; 'nyinyi' targets the interlocutor, not a group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kawaida ya waluhya they'll never unite' attributes collective, immutable failure to unite to the Luhya community as a whole - NCIC collective negative characteristic.

label: hate
target_group: Tanzanians / Africans broadly (implied Samia Suluhu's community)
rationale: 'Samia hakutakiwa kua Rais ni takataka' is primarily a personal insult but 'takataka' applied to a head of state read alongside commentary on foreign investors carries contempt; confidence low as the attack is arguably individual - picking lower severity would be offensive, but the ethnic/national dimension is unclear enough to note.

label: hate
rationale: 'wakikuyu hawakumpigia walikuwa wamepigia baba' holds Kikuyus collectively responsible for a specific electoral outcome and frames them as collectively conspiring, transferring political allegations onto the community as a whole.

label: neither
rationale: Criticism of a named political party (Kikuyu party) and its leaders as 'conmen'; targets politicians and their organisation, not the Kikuyu community collectively.

label: hate
rationale: 'how stupid my tribesmen get ikifika wakati wa elections' attributes collective electoral stupidity to fellow Kamba as a community - NCIC collective negative characteristic, self-directed but still group contempt.

label: offensive
rationale: 'umbwa' (dog) is crude personal abuse directed at an individual interlocutor; 'tutam' references a political faction but the insult is not grounded in ethnicity.

label: neither
rationale: 'wakabila mtalia saaaana' is pointed political taunting directed at political opponents and their sacco money, not an attack on any ethnic community's character as a whole.

label: offensive
rationale: 'tribal bigot per excellence' is a personal insult aimed at Gachagua as a named individual politician, not transferred onto his ethnic community.

label: hate
rationale: 'wajaa ngori kama waMeru' (full of cowardice like Meru people) attributes collective cowardice to the Meru community - NCIC collective negative characteristic.

label: hate
rationale: 'Islam is here to conquer... the true Trojan Horse. The true danger' declares the Muslim community collectively a subversive existential threat - NCIC collective contempt and ascription of treacherous intent to a religious group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Mafala nyinyi' (you fools) is generic personal abuse aimed at IEBC officials, no protected characteristic.

label: hate
rationale: 'very easy to manipulate. Hawajielewi' attributes collective gullibility and lack of self-awareness to Bukusus as a community - NCIC collective negative characteristic.

label: neither
rationale: Political denunciation of named politicians (Murkomen, Wanga, Mbadi, Omollo) for alleged betrayal; 'Luo Nation' is the audience being addressed, not a group being attacked.

label: neither
rationale: Sheng political banter about statehouse access and a named politician (Kabogo); 'cherargei' references Sifuna's identity but no contempt is directed at the Luhya or any community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'lacks ideas, zero development thinking, lazy and a tick' - personal abuse of named politician Kalonzo as an individual, no protected characteristic.
